title: sarracenia × farnhamii nothosubsp. farnhamii
intro: Sarracenia × farnhamii nothosubsp. farnhamii is a hybrid species of pitcher plant, resulting from the cross between Sarracenia alata and Sarracenia flava. The plant is known for its unique, cup-shaped leaves that serve as a carnivorous trap to capture insects for nutrition, which is essential due to its inability to obtain sufficient nutrients from the soil. Research suggests that Sarracenia × farnhamii nothosubsp. farnhamii contains medicinal properties such as antiviral and antibacterial compounds, which may be beneficial in treating various health conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is sometimes used to make teas and tinctures, which may help to boost the immune system and provide relief from respiratory issues due to its expectorant properties.
